---
title: "per_timepoint_fisher_aa — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-22
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [8, 22, 18, 16, 16, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![3x3 grid of BWM-vs-control amino-acid volcano panels: rows are timepoints (day 0 / day 5 / day 10) and columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Day-0 panels are richly populated and span the widest log2(OR) range; day-5 and day-10 panels collapse toward the origin.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (timepoint, site) BH-FDR family of 20; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR). Positive log2(OR) means BWM-enriched relative to control; negative means BWM-depleted.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `per_timepoint_fisher_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `per_timepoint_fisher`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `per_timepoint_fisher_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), BWM vs control 2x2 contingency per (timepoint, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (timepoint, site, aa) 2x2 BWM-vs-control contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 9 (timepoint, site) families of 20 AAs (180 tests total).

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition,timepoint) summed before Fisher's exact; the source script literally prints a pseudoreplication warning. Anti-conservative p-values.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are in the thousands (e.g. BWM_total ~6091, control_total ~27732 at day_0); Fisher's exact returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations (odds_ratio 1.36 at p=1e-10). odds_ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **imbalanced-N** — BWM_total ~6091 vs control_total ~27732 at day_0 (~5x asymmetry); Fisher handles imbalance fine but downstream interpretation should not over-read odds ratios when one cell is small.
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site)** — BH-FDR is computed within each (timepoint,site) family of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) tests. p_adj means "corrected within this slice", not across the whole CSV.

---

## Headline

Across 180 Fisher's exact tests (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 20 amino acids), 27 cells reach `p_adj` < 0.05 (15.0%) and 35 reach `p_adj` < 0.10 (19.4%). Eighteen of the FDR<0.05 hits sit at day 0 (A=7, P=5, E=6); day 5 contributes seven (A=1, P=1, E=5) and day 10 contributes two (A=1, P=0, E=1).

Looking across the time axis, 21 of 60 (site, aa) cells hold a single OR direction across all 3 timepoints (7 BWM-enriched, 14 BWM-depleted), and 39 show at least one sign change. Only E:K registers that sign change with `p_adj` < 0.05 on both opposite-sign rows: day 0 log2OR = +0.480 (`p_adj` = 2.87e-15, file minimum), day 5 log2OR = -0.195 (`p_adj` = 4.93e-3), and day 10 log2OR = -0.035 (ns).

The largest-magnitude FDR<0.05 hits cluster at day 0 — A:N -0.755, P:R +0.519, E:R +0.482, E:K +0.480, E:Q -0.467, P:P -0.460, A:K +0.439, A:R +0.381 — with one later-timepoint hit at day 10 A:A -0.415. Among the 21 direction-concordant cells, the most stable cross-timepoint signals are P:K (+0.253 mean, #sig 2/3), P:R (+0.239), A:R (+0.218), and A:T (-0.178).

The day-0 dominance should be read as power-modulated, not as direction-of-effect biology. The control:BWM count ratio is 4.55:1 at day 0, 0.94:1 at day 5, and 1.27:1 at day 10; the 12-orders-of-magnitude span in file-minimum `p_adj` across timepoints (2.87e-15 -> 4.93e-3 -> 1.86e-3) reflects asymmetric pooled N, not stronger biology at day 0.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (the log2 of the `odds_ratio` column). Direction is fixed by the BWM-vs-control contingency layout: positive log2(OR) = BWM-enriched, negative = BWM-depleted. `p_value` is the raw two-sided Fisher's exact p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs. Tables below merge all three ribosome sites and are split per-direction within each timepoint.

**Inclusion criterion.** An amino acid appears in a table only if its `p_adj` is below 0.05 within its own (timepoint, site) family of 20 — i.e. it clears BH-FDR correction inside that family. Every row clearing `p_adj` < 0.05 is shown (no row cap). Each table merges all three ribosome sites for a (timepoint, direction) pair; rows are grouped by `site` in A -> P -> E order and within each site sorted by `|log2_OR|` descending. If no rows clear the threshold for a given (timepoint, direction) combination, that table is omitted entirely.

Two cross-timepoint summary tables (direction concordance and direction-flip cells) appear under "Cross-timepoint summary" at the end of this section, after the per-(timepoint, direction) blocks.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site| amino acid| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---: |
| A| K (lysine)| +0.439| 1.16e-10| 2.31e-09| |
| A| R (arginine)| +0.381| 3.50e-06| 2.34e-05| |
| A| G (glycine)| +0.283| 1.35e-04| 6.72e-04| |
| P| R (arginine)| +0.519| 2.32e-10| 4.65e-09| |
| P| K (lysine)| +0.360| 1.05e-07| 1.05e-06| |
| P| G (glycine)| +0.195| 7.10e-03| 2.96e-02| |
| E| R (arginine)| +0.482| 1.09e-08| 1.09e-07| |
| E | K (lysine) | +0.480 | 1.43e-16 | 2.87e-15 | iid-amp |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site| amino acid| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---: |
| A| N (asparagine)| -0.755| 3.17e-09| 3.17e-08| |
| A| T (threonine)| -0.339| 1.50e-03| 5.98e-03| |
| A| F (phenylalanine)| -0.321| 5.90e-03| 1.98e-02| |
| A| D (aspartate)| -0.203| 1.47e-02| 4.21e-02| |
| P| P (proline)| -0.460| 1.95e-05| 1.30e-04| |
| P| S (serine)| -0.281| 7.40e-03| 2.96e-02| |
| E| Q (glutamine)| -0.467| 2.49e-05| 1.66e-04| |
| E| S (serine)| -0.375| 4.53e-04| 2.26e-03| |
| E| Y (tyrosine)| -0.371| 7.50e-03| 3.00e-02| |
| E | T (threonine) | -0.248 | 1.43e-02 | 4.78e-02 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site| amino acid| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---: |
| A| L (leucine)| +0.280| 2.49e-04| 4.98e-03| |
| P| K (lysine)| +0.211| 2.20e-03| 4.43e-02| |
| E| P (proline)| +0.358| 4.89e-04| 4.93e-03| |
| E | A (alanine) | +0.299 | 1.40e-03 | 9.35e-03 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site| amino acid| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---: |
| E| G (glycine)| -0.202| 8.30e-03| 3.32e-02| |
| E| K (lysine)| -0.195| 4.93e-04| 4.93e-03| |
| E | E (glutamate) | -0.174 | 7.20e-03 | 3.32e-02 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site| amino acid| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---: |
| E | R (arginine) | +0.388 | 9.29e-05 | 1.86e-03 | |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site| amino acid| effect (`log2_OR`)| raw p (`p_value`)| adjusted p (`p_adj`)| flag|
| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---:| :---: |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.415 | 1.45e-04 | 2.91e-03 | |


### Cross-timepoint summary (two ranking tables)

#### Cross-timepoint direction concordance

21 of 60 (site, aa) cells hold the same OR direction across all 3 timepoints. Below the cells are split by displayed mean-log2OR direction into BWM-enriched and BWM-depleted sub-tables; each is sorted by `#sig` descending, then by `|mean log2OR|` descending. `#sig` is the count (out of 3) of timepoints where the row reaches `p_adj` < 0.05 (the FDR-corrected threshold); `min p_adj` is the smallest of the 3 per-timepoint values. The `per-tp log2OR` column lists the three per-timepoint values in chronological order (day 0, day 5, day 10); `*` marks each per-timepoint value at `p_adj` < 0.05.

##### Concordance — BWM-enriched (mean log2OR > 0)

| Amino Acid | mean log2OR | per-tp log2OR | #sig | min p_adj | flag |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| P:K | +0.253 | +0.36\* +0.21\* +0.19 | 2/3 | 1.05e-06 | |
| P:R | +0.239 | +0.52\* +0.09 +0.10 | 1/3 | 4.65e-09 | |
| A:R | +0.218 | +0.38\* +0.13 +0.15 | 1/3 | 2.34e-05 | |
| A:W | +0.172 | +0.09 +0.12 +0.31 | 0/3 | 3.96e-01 | rare-aa (0, 5, 10) |
| A:H | +0.153 | +0.23 +0.11 +0.12 | 0/3 | 1.90e-01 | |
| P:L | +0.117 | +0.04 +0.11 +0.20 | 0/3 | 1.48e-01 | |
| E:L | +0.065 | +0.02 +0.15 +0.02 | 0/3 | 1.19e-01 | |

: {tbl-colwidths="[10, 10, 30, 14, 14, 22]"}

##### Concordance — BWM-depleted (mean log2OR < 0)

| Amino Acid | mean log2OR | per-tp log2OR | #sig | min p_adj | flag |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| A:T | -0.178 | -0.34\* -0.05 -0.14 | 1/3 | 5.98e-03 | |
| P:S | -0.136 | -0.28\* -0.11 -0.02 | 1/3 | 2.96e-02 | |
| E:E | -0.080 | -0.05 -0.17\* -0.02 | 1/3 | 3.32e-02 | |
| P:T | -0.166 | -0.22 -0.04 -0.23 | 0/3 | 7.07e-02 | |
| A:C | -0.146 | -0.08 -0.14 -0.22 | 0/3 | 4.95e-01 | rare-aa (0, 10) |
| P:A | -0.137 | -0.09 -0.13 -0.19 | 0/3 | 1.64e-01 | |
| P:C | -0.118 | -0.16 -0.12 -0.07 | 0/3 | 6.49e-01 | rare-aa (0, 10) |
| P:E | -0.108 | -0.19 -0.12 -0.01 | 0/3 | 5.59e-02 | |
| P:D | -0.091 | -0.10 -0.02 -0.15 | 0/3 | 1.64e-01 | |
| E:D | -0.084 | -0.12 -0.05 -0.08 | 0/3 | 3.47e-01 | |
| P:N | -0.049 | -0.09 -0.01 -0.05 | 0/3 | 5.72e-01 | |
| P:F | -0.046 | -0.01 -0.06 -0.07 | 0/3 | 7.34e-01 | |
| E:H | -0.045 | -0.04 -0.05 -0.04 | 0/3 | 7.43e-01 | |
| E:N | -0.043 | -0.04 -0.05 -0.04 | 0/3 | 5.96e-01 | |

: {tbl-colwidths="[10, 10, 30, 14, 14, 22]"}

#### Direction-flip cells across timepoints

39 of 60 (site, aa) cells show at least one sign change across the 3 timepoint values. Only 1 (E:K) has both opposite-sign rows reaching `p_adj` < 0.05 — flagged `both-flip-sig` below. `#sig` is the count (out of 3 timepoints) of rows reaching `p_adj` < 0.05 (the FDR-corrected threshold); the `per-tp log2OR` column lists the three per-timepoint values in chronological order (day 0, day 5, day 10); `*` marks each per-timepoint value that reaches the threshold. Sorted by `#sig` descending, then by max single-cell `|log2OR|` descending.

| Amino Acid | log2OR range | per-tp log2OR | #sig | flag |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| E:R | [-0.096, +0.482] | +0.48\* -0.10 +0.39\* | 2/3 | |
| E:K | [-0.195, +0.480] | +0.48\* -0.19\* -0.04 | 2/3 | both-flip-sig; iid-amp (0) |
| A:N | [-0.755, +0.297] | -0.75\* -0.25 +0.30 | 1/3 | |
| E:Q | [-0.467, +0.120] | -0.47\* -0.06 +0.12 | 1/3 | |
| P:P | [-0.460, +0.025] | -0.46\* +0.03 -0.22 | 1/3 | |
| A:K | [-0.062, +0.439] | +0.44\* -0.06 +0.13 | 1/3 | |
| A:A | [-0.415, +0.031] | -0.08 +0.03 -0.42\* | 1/3 | |
| E:S | [-0.375, +0.009] | -0.37\* +0.01 -0.02 | 1/3 | |
| E:Y | [-0.371, +0.242] | -0.37\* +0.24 +0.21 | 1/3 | |
| E:P | [-0.150, +0.358] | -0.15 +0.36\* -0.13 | 1/3 | |
| A:F | [-0.321, +0.117] | -0.32\* +0.12 -0.01 | 1/3 | |
| E:A | [-0.099, +0.299] | -0.08 +0.30\* -0.10 | 1/3 | |
| A:G | [-0.056, +0.283] | +0.28\* +0.02 -0.06 | 1/3 | |
| A:L | [-0.147, +0.280] | -0.02 +0.28\* -0.15 | 1/3 | |
| E:T | [-0.248, +0.219] | -0.25\* +0.22 -0.12 | 1/3 | |

: {tbl-colwidths="[14, 22, 30, 14, 20]"}

(15 highest-magnitude flip cells shown; 24 more flip cells below the cutoff (#sig, |log2OR|) = (1/3, 0.248) are in the data but not listed.)

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — single per-timepoint `p_adj` falls below 1e-10. The magnitude of `-log10(p_adj)` is inflated by Fisher's anti-conservative behaviour at large pooled N + correlated stall positions; rank by effect size, not by p magnitude.
- `rare-aa` — at least one of the 3 per-timepoint rows for this cell has `BWM_k` < 100 (or similarly low `ctrl_k`). Odds-ratio estimate is unstable; large |log2OR| values may reflect sampling noise rather than biology.
- `both-flip-sig` — direction-flip cell where both opposite-sign timepoint rows reach `p_adj` < 0.05; the cell records a genuine FDR-significant sign change rather than a sub-threshold near-zero crossing. Only E:K qualifies in this file.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 180 (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 20 AAs)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 27 (15.0%)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 35 (19.4%)
- `min adjusted-p`: 2.87e-15 (day_0 E K, log2OR=+0.480, BWM_k=983/n=6091, ctrl_k=3362/n=27732)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher's exact has no analytic floor; effective discreteness floor at small (k_bwm + k_ctrl) is captured by `small-bh-family-discreteness`
- Cells with p_adj < 1e-10: 1 of 180 (only day_0 E K); flagged `iid-amp`

### Per-(timepoint, site) hit counts and pooled totals

Each `A` / `P` / `E` cell shows `n_sig / n_tests` — the number of amino acids whose `p_adj` clears 0.05 within that (timepoint, site) BH-FDR family of 20. `BWM_total` and `control_total` are the pooled stall counts that feed every 2x2 contingency in the row, and `ctrl:BWM ratio` is the per-row N asymmetry that drives Fisher's effective power. Day-0 carries a 4.55:1 control:BWM imbalance, day-5 is near-balanced at 0.94:1, and day-10 sits at 1.27:1 — the same underlying effect size therefore lands at very different `p_adj` magnitudes across rows (see the `n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction` caveat). Read this table for power context, not as a direction-of-effect summary.

| timepoint | A | P | E | BWM total | control total | ctrl:BWM ratio |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| day_0 | 7/20 | 5/20 | 6/20 | 6091 | 27732 | 4.55 |
| day_5 | 1/20 | 1/20 | 5/20 | 11935 | 11177 | 0.94 |
| day_10 | 1/20 | 0/20 | 1/20 | 6945 | 8788 | 1.27 |
: {tbl-colwidths="[18, 8, 8, 8, 19, 19, 20]"}

- Cross-timepoint (site, aa) cells, all 3 timepoints same OR direction: 21/60 (7 BWM-enriched, 14 BWM-depleted)
- Cross-timepoint (site, aa) cells with at least one sign change: 39/60
- Cells with sign change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows: 1 (E:K)

## Biological interpretation

**Day-0 BWM ribosomes are enriched for basic residues (K, R) at all three ribosome sites and depleted for asparagine at the A site (concrete).** Both Lys and Arg show large, FDR-confirmed BWM-enrichment at all three sites at day 0 — E:K (+0.48, p_adj=2.9e-15), E:R (+0.48), P:R (+0.52), P:K (+0.36), A:K (+0.44), A:R (+0.38). Co-enrichment of K and R at the A site is consistent with electrostatic slowing in the exit tunnel: positively charged nascent-chain residues interact with the negatively charged ribosomal RNA backbone and slow elongation through the A/P/E corridor. The mirror image at day 0 A site is N (-0.76 at p_adj=3.2e-8) — depletion of a small polar residue is harder to anchor mechanistically and may reflect compositional displacement by the over-represented basics rather than an N-specific stalling effect.

**The day-0 P-site proline depletion is mechanistically anchored (concrete).** P-site P at day 0 is depleted at log2(OR) = -0.46 (p_adj=1.3e-4). Pro is the canonical exit-tunnel stalling residue (eIF5A/EF-P-dependent rescue): if BWM has *fewer* stalls with P at the P site than control, the BWM stall population is shifted away from poly-Pro stall events at day 0 specifically. This is one of the few day-0 hits whose direction (depletion in BWM) cuts against a "BWM has more conventional stall events" reading, and is worth flagging at the synthesis layer.

**The K-at-E flip between day 0 and day 5 is the file's only FDR-significant direction change (exploratory).** E:K is BWM-enriched at day 0 (+0.48, p_adj=2.9e-15, the file minimum) and BWM-depleted at day 5 (-0.20, p_adj=4.9e-3). Both rows clear FDR<0.05 in opposite directions, and the d0 row is flagged `iid-amp` — meaning the p magnitude is amplified by large-N anti-conservatism (control:BWM ratio 4.55:1 at day 0). Treating this as a real biphasic Lys-at-E phenomenon would require corroboration in the codon-resolution sister CSV (does AAG-at-E or AAA-at-E flip in the same direction?) and in the within-condition Fisher contrasts (is the d0->d5 step the carrier, or is the per-timepoint design picking up a small-effect crossing zero?). Hold as a hypothesis, not a finding.

**The strong day-0 dominance is largely a power story, not a biology story (concrete).** Per-(timepoint, site) hit counts collapse from 18 at day 0 to 7 at day 5 to 2 at day 10, while file-minimum `p_adj` spans 12 orders of magnitude over the same gradient (2.9e-15 -> 4.9e-3 -> 1.9e-3). The control:BWM N ratio is 4.55:1 at day 0, 0.94:1 at day 5, 1.27:1 at day 10 — moderate effect sizes at day 0 inflate to extreme p magnitudes that the same effect sizes at later timepoints would not reach. The `n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction` caveat applies: per-cell hit-count differences are power-modulated, not evidence that BWM-control divergence is concentrated at day 0 in biology.

**A subset of cross-timepoint concordant cells points at stable BWM-vs-control composition differences (exploratory).** P:K (+0.25 mean, sig in 2/3 timepoints), P:R (+0.24), A:R (+0.22), and A:T (-0.18) are direction-consistent across all three timepoints with moderate magnitudes. The basic-residue enrichment direction matches the day-0 headline but holds across the time axis at smaller magnitudes — consistent with a stable BWM-vs-control compositional offset on top of which the day-0 amplification rides. These cells are worth carrying into the codon sister CSV (do they aggregate from AGA/CGC/CGT at codon resolution?) and the within-condition binomial (are they baseline features shared by both conditions, or BWM-specific?).

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the day-0 basic-residue enrichment (K/R at A/P/E) and P-site Pro depletion; the day_10 P-site has no statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 and the day_10 A and E sites each carry only one FDR<0.05 hit. *Exploratory*: the E:K direction flip (needs codon-level and within-condition corroboration); the 21-cell cross-timepoint concordant set as a stable BWM-control offset hypothesis. *Noise*: the day-10 P-site row is flat (min p_adj = 0.148) and contributes nothing; A:N at day 0 (-0.755) is the file's largest depletion magnitude in the FDR<0.05 set, but its Top hits row carries no flag and it appears in the Direction-flip table at range [-0.755, +0.297] with only 1/3 FDR-sig — read it as a single-timepoint hit, not a stable cross-timepoint feature.

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) on a 2x2 contingency BWM_count vs control_count for each (timepoint, site, aa), with BH-FDR correction within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs. Effect column is `odds_ratio` (re-expressed as log2OR in this report's tables for symmetry with sister files); test statistic and exact p are returned by `scipy.stats.fisher_exact`. The p-correction column is `p_adj` (BH per (timepoint, site) family). The test answers "is the BWM-vs-control AA composition at this site different at this timepoint?". It does *not* answer "is each condition's per-aa enrichment vs background informative" (that is the `within_condition_binomial` family) and *not* "is the BWM-vs-control difference itself shifting across timepoints" (that requires a 3-way model or an explicit between-timepoint contrast — see the `between_timepoint_wilcoxon` family for the within-condition variant of that question).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `odds_ratio` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** With many thousands of pooled stall events and stalls correlated on shared motifs/transcripts, Fisher's effective N is smaller than `k_total`; treat tiny p magnitudes (`p_adj` < 1e-10) as inflated and rank by `log2_OR`.
- **imbalanced-N.** At day 0, control_total (27732) dwarfs BWM_total (6091); Fisher concentrates its information in the larger arm and inflates `p_adj` at moderate effects — read day-0 magnitudes against day-5/day-10 with the imbalance in mind, not as a count of "more biology."
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 9 (timepoint, site) families of 20; two cells with the same raw p in different cells can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (timepoint, site) cell, not across the full 180-test grid.
- **small-bh-family-discreteness.** At family size 20 BH adjacency steps are coarse and `p_adj` values cluster at a small number of distinct levels within a single cell (day_5,A has 5 rows tied at `p_adj` = 0.0517 and below; day_10,P top of family at `p_adj` = 0.148) — treat the 0.05 threshold as a binary flag, not a precise rank.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Rare AAs (W, M, C and several others by cell) have `BWM_k` or `ctrl_k` under 100 in some cells; OR estimates and CIs are unstable at small k. Flagged rows are reported but the magnitude weight should account for the count.
- **OR-direction-anchor.** OR > 1 = BWM-enriched, OR < 1 = BWM-depleted at this (timepoint, site, aa); stated here so downstream consumers do not invert the direction.
- **n-asymmetry-affects-power-not-direction.** The 12-OOM gap between min `p_adj` at day 0 (2.87e-15) and day 5 (4.93e-3) or day 10 (1.86e-3) is consistent with day 0's 4.55:1 control:BWM ratio amplifying moderate effects; per-cell hit counts are power-modulated, not direction-of-effect biology.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-0 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: log2(OR) on x, -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05. Lysine (K, basic blue) at +0.44 log2(OR) and asparagine (N, polar purple) at -0.76 are the largest-magnitude FDR<0.05 hits; arginine (R, basic blue) and glycine (G, neutral orange) are also enriched; threonine (T, polar), phenylalanine (F, hydrophobic green), and aspartate (D, acidic red) are modestly depleted.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_A_volcano.png)

![Day-0 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: arginine (R, basic blue) at +0.52 log2(OR) and lysine (K, basic) at +0.36 are the top-enriched FDR<0.05 hits; proline (P, neutral orange) at -0.46 is the strongest depletion; glycine (G, neutral) and serine (S, polar) are marginally significant.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_P_volcano.png)

![Day-0 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: lysine (K, basic blue) at +0.48 log2(OR), p_adj=2.9e-15 sits highest on the y-axis (file-minimum); arginine (R, basic) is also enriched; glutamine (Q, polar), serine (S, polar), tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green), and threonine (T, polar) are FDR<0.05 depletions.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: nearly all points collapse to the origin; leucine (L, hydrophobic green) at +0.28 log2(OR) is the only FDR<0.05 hit. Scale and dashed FDR/effect-size guides are identical to the day-0 panels for cross-timepoint comparability.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: a flat panel; lysine (K, basic blue) at +0.21 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit, sitting just above the FDR threshold line.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: 5 FDR<0.05 hits clustered tightly around the origin; proline (P, neutral orange) and alanine (A, hydrophobic green) are enriched (+0.30 to +0.36 log2(OR)); lysine (K, basic blue), glycine (G, neutral), and glutamate (E, acidic red) are depleted (-0.17 to -0.20).](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: alanine (A, hydrophobic green) at -0.42 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit; all other amino acids collapse below the FDR threshold line within the +/-0.3 log2(OR) band.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: no FDR<0.05 hits; all points fall below the FDR threshold and within the +/-0.4 log2(OR) band — the flattest panel in the file (min `p_adj` = 0.148).](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: arginine (R, basic blue) at +0.39 log2(OR) is the single FDR<0.05 hit; all other amino acids fall below the FDR threshold.](../../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_E_volcano.png)
